title: If End of Evangelion had an ending song
display_title: If End of Evangelion had an ending song
wikitext:
[]: If End of Evangelion had an ending song...             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:05 pm I think the way End of Evangelion's ending is perfect the way it is. However, if you had to give it an ending song, which one would you give? My first choice is the strings version of Jesus bleibet meine Freude . This was found on some releases of EoE like the laserdisc one. My other choice is Star from Evangelion Vox. It's weird, but I think the theme really fits EoE in a weird way. And apparently, according to the album, its melody is based on the next episode theme, which makes it fitting to put it on the last episode of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:07 pm Everything You Ever Dreamed would be my choice.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:13 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: Everything You Ever Dreamed would be my choice.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:16 pm Everything You Ever Dreamed would be good. Or, if you wanted to add some more classical music to the OST, Chopin's Raindrop Prelude.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:18 pm Best way to end any bittersweet movie.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:20 pm Stryker wrote: Best way to end any bittersweet movie.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:25 pm Service, Service! the heady feeling of freedom could also be good.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:43 pm Everything you've ever dreamed (which i think was planned as the ending song in fact before they decided to put the subtitles in the middle) Not entirely decided on whether the english or Japanese lyrics would be better. Either that, or that music that Shinji was listening in his walkman so much: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oaLOOiXbkYo[/url] EDIT: Thanks for the advice Reichu. The song seems to be called "You are the only one" by a so called Lilia. Reason why i thought it might be appropiate for an ending credit is that, if i recall correctly, it always seemed to play whenever a significant event for Shinji was ocurring (A part in ep4 and the kiss in ep 15 particularly come to mind)

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:51 pm Dream wrote: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oaLOOiXbkYo[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 27, 2012 8:17 pm YouTube links die easily, so for longevity's sake it might be better to mention the name of the song that you're linking to in your post.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Oct 27, 2012 8:21 pm riffraff11235 wrote: BRILLIANT! I'm pretty sure that would increase the mind-fuckery a few notches. A promise of more "service" at the end would help as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sat Oct 27, 2012 8:23 pm I'm a bit confused - Is this thread about a song to replace 'Thanatos' roll credits, or music for the very final scene? If it's the latter, I'd leave it as it is. The solemn sound of the waves lapping on the beach is perfect BGM.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Oct 27, 2012 8:27 pm Blue Monday wrote: I'm a bit confused - Is this thread about a song to replace 'Thanatos' roll credits, or music for the very final scene? If it's the latter, I'd leave it as it is. The solemn sound of the waves lapping on the beach is perfect BGM.

[]:             Quote Post by StratoSakuya...AF » Sat Oct 27, 2012 8:31 pm riffraff11235 wrote: BRILLIANT! I'm pretty sure that would increase the mind-fuckery a few notches. A promise of more "service" at the end would help as well.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Oct 28, 2012 4:37 am Funny... On my german "The End of Evangelion" DVD there IS a credit roll after "One More Final".... I can't remember the song though, and the DVD's not in my apartment... But as you said... If One More Final just fades away without credits... I has a much greater impact!

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Oct 28, 2012 4:44 am robersora wrote: Funny... On my german "The End of Evangelion" DVD there IS a credit roll after "One More Final".... I can't remember the song though, and the DVD's not in my apartment...

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Oct 28, 2012 5:18 am ^ Oh, you're right.... It IS crappy and it was Thanatos again. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Oct 28, 2012 4:32 pm I really like the idea of Star. It would be so dissonant with the movie, but it fits.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sun Oct 28, 2012 6:29 pm Clover wrote: I really like the idea of Star. It would be so dissonant with the movie, but it fits.

[]:             Quote Post by evangelist » Sun Oct 28, 2012 10:59 pm Hope they bring back Fly me to the Moon.

And apparently, according to the album, its melody is based on the next episode theme, which makes it fitting to put it on the last episode of the series.
